description:
You are performing a comprehensive structural analysis of drug binding to BRAF kinase for a drug design project. This multi-part task requires you to characterize the vemurafenib binding site, compare DFG-in vs DFG-out kinase conformations, compute drug-protein buried surface area, and model a gatekeeper resistance mutation.

Part 1 - Vemurafenib Binding Site Characterization:
Fetch PDB 3OG7 (BRAF V600E kinase bound to vemurafenib). Identify the small-molecule ligand. Select all protein residues that have any heavy atom within 4.0 Angstroms of the ligand to define the binding pocket. Record the complete list of pocket-lining residues with residue number and three-letter name. Classify each pocket residue as hydrophobic (Ala, Val, Leu, Ile, Phe, Trp, Met, Pro), polar (Ser, Thr, Tyr, Asn, Gln, Cys, His, Gly), or charged (Asp, Glu, Arg, Lys), and report the count for each category. Measure the following five C-alpha to nearest ligand heavy atom distances in Angstroms: (1) Thr529, (2) Phe595, (3) Cys532, (4) Glu501 CA to Lys483 CA, (5) Gly466. Display polar contacts (hydrogen bonds) between the protein and the ligand. Save a publication-quality ray-traced figure (minimum 1200x900 resolution) showing the protein as gray cartoon, ligand as green sticks, pocket residues as cyan lines, and H-bonds as yellow dashes to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/braf_vemurafenib_pocket.png

Part 2 - DFG Conformational Dynamics:
Fetch PDB 1UWH (BRAF bound to sorafenib, DFG-out conformation) into the same PyMOL session. Align 1UWH onto 3OG7 using the kinase domain and record the alignment RMSD. Important: the two PDB entries use different residue numbering conventions, so after alignment you must identify structurally equivalent residues by their amino acid type and sequence context rather than by residue number alone. Locate the DFG motif (Asp-Phe-Gly sequence) in each structure and measure the displacement of the DFG-motif phenylalanine C-alpha between the aligned structures. Similarly locate the conserved alpha-C-helix glutamate in each structure and measure its C-alpha displacement between the aligned structures. Identify residues in 1UWH within 4.0 Angstroms of sorafenib that are NOT within 4.0 Angstroms of vemurafenib in 3OG7 - these are back-pocket residues uniquely accessible in the DFG-out state. List these back-pocket residues. Save a comparison figure showing both structures as cartoon (3OG7 in gray, 1UWH in light blue) with the DFG motif residues displayed as sticks in both conformations and both ligands as sticks, to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/braf_dfg_comparison.png

Part 3 - Drug Buried Surface Area:
Using the 3OG7 structure, compute the solvent-accessible surface area (SASA) of the binding pocket residues (those identified in Part 1 as within 4.0 Angstroms of the ligand). First compute SASA of those pocket residues with the ligand removed or hidden from the structure. Then compute SASA of those same residues with the ligand present. The drug-buried surface area (BSA) equals SASA_without_ligand minus SASA_with_ligand. Report all three values. Create a surface representation of the binding pocket colored by residue property: hydrophobic residues in yellow, polar in white, positively charged in blue, negatively charged in red. Save to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/braf_pocket_surface.png

Part 4 - Gatekeeper Resistance Mutation:
On the 3OG7 structure, use the PyMOL mutagenesis wizard to mutate Thr529 to methionine (Met). Choose a rotamer. Measure the closest distance between any Met529 sidechain heavy atom and any ligand heavy atom. Also measure Met529 CA to nearest ligand heavy atom distance. Assess whether the methionine sidechain creates a steric clash with the ligand (distance below 2.5 Angstroms indicates clash). Save a close-up figure showing the mutation site with the mutant Met529 sidechain visible near the ligand to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/braf_gatekeeper_mutation.png

Part 5 - Comprehensive Report:
Write a structured analysis report to ~/PyMOL_Data/braf_drug_analysis_report.txt with these clearly labeled sections:
SECTION 1 - BINDING POCKET: Total pocket residue count, complete residue list (number and name for each), and classification counts for hydrophobic, polar, and charged residues.
SECTION 2 - KEY DISTANCES: All five distance measurements from Part 1 with labels and values in Angstroms.
SECTION 3 - DFG DYNAMICS: Alignment RMSD value, DFG-Phe CA displacement between the two structures, alpha-C-helix Glu CA displacement, and list of back-pocket residues unique to the DFG-out state.
SECTION 4 - BURIED SURFACE AREA: SASA without ligand, SASA with ligand, and the computed BSA value, all in square Angstroms.
SECTION 5 - GATEKEEPER MUTATION: Met529 closest distance to ligand, Met529 CA distance to ligand, and steric clash assessment with determination (yes or no).
SECTION 6 - DRUG DESIGN IMPLICATIONS: Based on your structural evidence, discuss how the DFG-out conformation creates an extended binding pocket and how the gatekeeper mutation may confer resistance to vemurafenib.
Save the PyMOL session to ~/PyMOL_Data/sessions/braf_analysis.pse
